# `energy/state_power_availability_mu`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: State-wise availability of power (MU)  
**One-line**: Annual availability of energy in the state (MU = million units = GWh), fiscal year.  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_power_availability_mu.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_power_availability_mu.json)

## Definition

Annual availability of energy in the state (MU = million units = GWh), fiscal year. 'Availability' is the energy actually supplied to the state grid after accounting for inter-state imports / exports and transmission losses — i.e. what was put on the bus for distribution companies to sell to consumers. Read alongside Power Requirement (T141) — the gap between requirement and availability is the 'energy not supplied' (deficit), which CEA tracks separately.

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2004-04..2024-04 (21 periods)
- **Spatial**: India (states + UTs); 34 entities (34 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 648

## Methodology vintage

RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian States 2024-25 edition; originating data Central Electricity Authority, Ministry of Power.

## Notes

Source: RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian States 2024-25 edition, Table 139. Originating data: Central Electricity Authority. Note that 'availability' is a transmission-side metric, not consumption — for actual electricity sold to end users see ICED-derived `energy/state_electricity_sales_mu.json`.
